summary: The test suite is comprehensive and well-organized, covering all five task requirements: process record creation (New-ProcessRecord), threshold filtering (Test-ProcessThreshold, Get-FilteredProcesses), top-N ranking (Get-TopProcesses), report generation (New-AlertReport), and end-to-end integration (Invoke-ProcessMonitor). Fixtures via BeforeEach are reused cleanly, script-scope variables are used correctly, and TDD intent is clearly annotated with RED/GREEN comments. Rigor is strong — empty inputs, invalid sort fields, missing hashtable keys, zero thresholds, and TopN exceeding list length are all covered. The main gaps preventing a 5 in rigor are: (1) the boundary condition where a value equals exactly the threshold is never tested (unclear whether it passes or fails), (2) no tests for null/empty process names, (3) no test for what Invoke-ProcessMonitor produces when doom appears (CpuPercent=60 > 50 threshold) — the integration test only asserts chrome appears and idle does not, leaving doom's presence/absence unverified. Despite these minor omissions, the suite is trustworthy for catching regressions across all core behaviors.
